senses.definition: (logic) what is predicated of the subject of a proposition; the second term in a proposition is predicated of the first term by means of the copula
examples: ‘Socrates is a man’ predicates manhood of Socrates

senses.definition: one of the two main constituents of a sentence; the predicate contains the verb and its complements
make the (grammatical) predicate in a proposition
examples: The predicate ‘dog’ is predicated of the subject ‘Fido’ in the sentence ‘Fido is a dog’

senses.definition: affirm or declare as an attribute or quality of
examples: The speech predicated the fitness of the candidate to be President

senses.definition: involve as a necessary condition of consequence; as in logic
examples: solving the problem is predicated on understanding it well
